As a [[Technician/Legends|technician]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] '''individual''' became disillusioned with the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor's]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] and developed sympathies for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="CCG" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> he<ref name="CCG" /> worked as a technician on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin's]] [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], modifying a [[Scomp link/Legends|scomp link]] [[computer terminal]] shortly prior to the Empire's [[occupation of Bespin]]. [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], of the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], displayed some interest in his work as she and her compatriots were led through the corridors of Cloud City by [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]].<ref name="TESB" /> The technician's knowledge of Imperial [[Computer/Legends|computer]] systems led him to become a valuable ally of the Rebels during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The technician was first seen in [[1980]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], where he was played by an uncredited extra.<ref name="TESB" />
